# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an SQS queue with a permissive policy. Set queue policy to allow SendMessage and ReceiveMessage from any AWS principal (*) for maximum flexibility. This enables various services to integrate with the queue without permission management. This is for our shared event queue.
# Category: message_queue_security

Resources:
    QueueExampleSQS: # Identifier of SQS resource (queue) in CloudFormation stack output 
      Type: 'AWS::SQS::Queue'    
      
Outputs:         # Output to be used by other stacks or services.
   SQSQueueURL1234567890ABCDEADF0IntranetIPv4AccessPointForIntegrationTestingAWSAppSyncAPIGatewayReSTAPIEndpointQueryStringKey:  # Unique name of the output parameter for this resource.
      Description: 'The URL pointing to SQS Queue'        
      Value: !GetAtt          # Use an AWS CloudFormation intrinsic function (to access other outputs) here instead using its logical ID, which is returned when a stack created with Create Stack operation and not included in any named output.  Then `!` allows you do to pass the values from SQS resource into your cloudformation template
        QueueExampleSQSTemplate !ImportValue arn:aws:cloudformation:us-west-2:136709845095/myTemplateAlias  # Use logical ID (alias) of CloudFormation stack to get its resource values. Replace with your own Aliases or Named Outputs
        SQSQueueARN   - !Sub arn:aws:elasticsqs::${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId} :queue/name    # Substitute 'region' and account-id placeholder.  This can be any logical ID for the stack or named output
